subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Neighbors as First Responders
description: A quick, practical guide to Neighbors as First Responders.

hook.text: After the 1985 Mexico City earthquake, civilians rescued more people than all agencies combined.

buildup.text: Professional responders take time to mobilize. Neighbors are already there.

discovery.text: Community Emergency Response Teams train citizens in basic search, rescue, and medical triage.

twist.text: Studies show that 90% of disaster survivors are rescued by untrained neighbors, not professionals.

climax.text: Knowing your neighbors' skills, needs, and vulnerabilities multiplies community resilience.

punchline.text: Your first responder lives next door. Do you know their name?

quiz.question: What percentage of disaster survivors are rescued by neighbors, not professionals?
